rationale: The cited 2009 paper is directly about how ease of computing price differences changes perceived magnitude and choice, which is conceptually close to the citing 2019 paper’s price-framing mechanism work. The citing note explicitly says it includes robustness tests for “easy computations” and positions itself as refining prior mechanism accounts in price-presentation research, suggesting the citation could be theoretically substantive rather than incidental. With a verified DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.46.1.81), this pair should be retained for full relation assessment.
